title: Remove legacy claude.* settings fallbacks after models.* migration soaks
description: Cut over the claude.*→models.* settings migration: remove legacy claude.* read fallbacks from allowlisted files, delete the .claude.* lint allowlist + no-restricted-properties rule, drop the compat claude IPC schema field (bump protocol version), and increment MODELS_NAMESPACE_STORE_VERSION. Gated on a clean 30-day migration audit (settingsMigrationDegraded <1%, no models-namespace exception spikes, no open support tickets).

provenance.notes: Migrated from the old per-push check-cleanup-deadline gate. Full cut-over checklist (sites, lint allowlist entries, IPC schema, store-version bump) lives in the plan doc's 'Inputs from 260505 canonical accessor' section.

history.reason: Original deadline came due with cleanup still pending; ~2-week stopgap at greg's explicit request to unblock dev pushes. NOT a confirmation audit criteria are met — owner must re-evaluate the 30-day trigger + audit by the new deadline.

Re-defer the RESIDUAL only. The substantive cut-over IS DONE + soaked: C2a materialization migration (store v2) + C2b runtime claude.* read-fallback removal shipped in v0.4.47 (2026-06-11..15) and v0.4.48 stable production; 14d Sentry clean (zero migration:models-namespace exceptions, zero settingsMigrationDegraded). Runtime is models-only — no genuine runtime claude readers remain. What's LEFT is C2c (full inert-field deletion: claude? off AppSettings type+Zod schema, materializer legacy branch, last 2 .claude.* eslint allowlist entries, IPC protocol bump). Deferring C2c: MEDIUM value (plan itself calls it 'cheap forensic insurance', not urgent) vs real cross-surface risk — the one-time migration machinery (migrateClaudeToModelsNamespace, withDefaultInjectedModelsStripped guard, codex-repair lockstep-clear, materializer) still READS raw settings.claude for v0/v1 installs that skip 0.4.47/0.4.48 and upgrade later. Deleting the type/schema makes that live legacy bridge MORE implicit/fragile for tidiness gain only. GPT-5.5-extra-high cross-family review: rec C, conf 82, biggest risk = stale v0/v1 upgrader gets electron-store default-injected models winning over real legacy claude. GATE for next attempt: a dedicated stale-upgrader (pre-cutover settings -> latest) safety proof/test before deletion, NOT just calendar soak; land with the 2 plan-flagged construction guards (normalizeSettings unguarded-materializer; models-only-readers always-materialized invariant). See docs/plans/260604_models_namespace_migration_cleanup/PLAN.md Final Summary + Decision Log.

title: Converge runtime/provider test fixtures onto a canonical models-emitting AppSettings builder
description: Several integration/runtime/live-api tests hand-roll AppSettings with legacy claude.* auth and no models.* block (e.g. tests/live-api/anthropicDirect.live.integration.test.ts). Runtime auth is models-only since C2b-1 (0cef1cb681), so hand-rolled fixtures silently rot when the settings namespace changes (modelIdLifecycle.integration.test.ts went red on dev this way). Converge these fixtures onto a shared models-emitting builder (cf. providerResolutionHarness.modelSettings()). Also note: integration tests are CI-gated (vitest --project=desktop, blocking) but NOT pre-push-gated (pre-push is VITEST_FAST + related, which excludes *.integration.*), so cross-cutting refactors can land integration breakage that only CI catches post-merge.

provenance.notes: Surfaced during CE2 modelIdLifecycle fix 2026-06-07; reviewer F2. Don't add a claude-namespace AST guard (C2c deletes the namespace) or run integration tests in pre-push (slows the fast gate).

history.reason: Surfaced during CE2 modelIdLifecycle fix 2026-06-07; reviewer F2. Don't add a claude-namespace AST guard (C2c deletes the namespace) or run integration tests in pre-push (slows the fast gate).
